title: CachingStore: correct lock discipline — mutex never held across .await
description: CachingStore correctly releases the Mutex before any async call. The pattern (acquire lock, copy data, drop lock, then call inner backend) is the right way to use Mutex with async code. The thundering-herd behavior on TTL expiry is documented accurately. A contributor might suggest 'simplify by holding the lock across the inner.get() call to prevent races' — this must be rejected because holding a sync Mutex across .await will deadlock under tokio's current-thread scheduler when another task tries to acquire the same lock.
